Advanced Hospital Admissions Visual Analysis

The main view is a small-multiple age-year heatmap for diagnosis categories, supported by matrix, radial, parallel, and age-composition views. Year checkboxes dynamically change every view.

Years to include

Small multiples: age-group trends by fiscal year

Level: primary diagnosis summary · Columns: fiscal years · Rows: 0-14, 15-59, 60-74, 75+
Primary diagnosis summary categories are shown with code + description. Fiscal years run across columns, age groups run down rows, and 3-character / 4-character diagnosis levels are intentionally excluded from this view.
This is the main view for research question C. Each panel is one primary diagnosis summary category labeled with both code and description. Cell color shows age-specific admissions counts within that category using a panel-specific scale, so age-pattern changes remain visible even when total burden differs sharply across categories. The source tables provide age splits for admissions, not age-specific FCE or bed days, so this view remains fixed to admissions while the supporting views switch metric.

Parallel coordinates: multi-attribute profile

Level: ICD-10 chapter
Show or hide chapters
Each chapter becomes a path across burden, emergency share, mean age, length of stay, and 75+ share. Crossings reveal relationships and exceptions.

Matrix heatmap: chapter x fiscal year

Level: ICD-10 chapter · Columns: fiscal years
Rows are ICD-10 chapters, columns are selected years, color is intensity. This is better than many lines because it shows all categories at once and highlights the lockdown break.

Radial year-ring matrix

Level: ICD-10 chapter · Rings: fiscal years
Rings are years, sectors are chapters. The 2020-21 ring makes system-wide disruption visible as a circular anomaly.

Age-composition matrix

Level: primary diagnosis summary · Columns: 0-14, 15-59, 60-74, 75+
Rows are high-burden detailed categories, columns are age groups. Color shows share within category, making age disparity visible without separate charts.

Discovery table: largest detailed categories for the selected years

Level: primary diagnosis summary category
CategoryChapterAdmissionsEmergency shareMean ageMean stay
